name: Milwaukee to Newark LTL
hero-headline: Milwaukee, WI to Newark, NJ LTL Freight
subheadline: 854 miles. 3-4 business day transit. 4 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Milwaukee to Newark

The Milwaukee to Newark LTL lane covers approximately 854 miles between the Midwest and Northeast regions. Standard transit runs 3-4 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Chicago Gateway, Indianapolis Hub, Columbus Terminal support consolidation and transit optimization. Winter weather impacts Dec-Feb. Agricultural peaks Jul-Oct.

How WARP Operates the Milwaukee to Newark Lane

WARP covers the Milwaukee to Newark corridor with 4 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 3-4 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at Chicago Gateway and Indianapolis Hub enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 854 miles (Milwaukee to Newark)
  • Transit window: 3-4 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 4
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: Chicago Gateway, Indianapolis Hub, Columbus Terminal, NY/NJ Metro Hub, Philadelphia Terminal
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Milwaukee to Newark lane range from $2,980 to $3,973, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 854 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $4.
  • 07/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Milwaukee to Newark LTL (854-mile corridor; 3–4 day transit window; 4 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at Chicago Gateway and Indianapolis Hub). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Milwaukee, WI to Newark, NJ cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $2,980 to $3,973 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Milwaukee to Newark?

Standard LTL transit on this 854-mile lane is 3-4 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Milwaukee to Newark lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 4 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Milwaukee to Newark in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Milwaukee to Newark corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
